source_paper_title: Robust Sparsification for Matroid Intersection with Applications
context:
Page 6, Section 1 (Introduction), paragraph beginning “Moreover, in our algorithms, as we frequently update the ground set …” in the discussion of density-based decomposition and principal partitions

Moreover, in our algorithms, as we frequently update the ground set on which we compute the decomposition by adding or removing one element, there may be room to improve our time complexity: we leave as an open question whether updating a density-based decomposition when performing these kinds of operations can be done more efficiently, without re-computing the whole decomposition each time.
title: Efficient dynamic update of the density-based decomposition of a matroid under single-element insertions/deletions
latex:
Given a matroid \(M=(V,\mathcal{I})\) with rank function \(\mathrm{rank}_M:2^V\to\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}\), and a subset \(V'\subseteq V\), let \(M|_{V'}\) denote the restriction of \(M\) to \(V'\). Define the density of a nonempty set \(U\subseteq V'\) as \(\rho(U)=\frac{|U|}{\mathrm{rank}_{M|_{V'}}(U)}\) (and \(\rho(\emptyset)=0\)). The density-based decomposition of \(V'\) is the sequence \(U_1,\dots,U_k\) (where \(k=\mathrm{rank}_M(V)\)) obtained by the greedy rule: \(U_1\) is the (unique) maximum-cardinality subset of \(V'\) maximizing \(\rho\) in \(M|_{V'}\); for \(j\ge 2\), \(U_j\) is the maximum-cardinality subset maximizing density in the contracted matroid \((M|_{V'})/(U_1\cup\cdots\cup U_{j-1})\), and the process continues until \(V'=U_1\cup\cdots\cup U_k\) (allowing empty sets).

Assume oracle access to \(M\) (e.g., an independence or rank oracle). Design and analyze an algorithm/data structure that maintains the density-based decomposition \(U_1,\dots,U_k\) of the current subset \(V'\subseteq V\) under an online sequence of updates of the form:
- insertion: \(V'\leftarrow V'\cup\{e\}\) for some \(e\in V\setminus V'\),
- deletion: \(V'\leftarrow V'\setminus\{e\}\) for some \(e\in V'\),
so that after each update the maintained decomposition is exactly the density-based decomposition of the new \(V'\).

The goal is to achieve an update time asymptotically smaller than recomputing the entire decomposition from scratch after each update (e.g., sublinear in \(|V|\) or in the time of a full recomputation), while using polynomial (or near-linear) space and a number of oracle queries per update that is similarly improved over full recomputation.
